title: The war on drugs is the number one enemy to civilization

transcript.text: What is the number one enemy to civilization?
I think the number one enemy to civilization is the war on drugs.
That is the number one enemy to civilization.
Because, number one, you're fighting something, marijuana,
That has been around since the beginning of time.
We've used 80% of it.
I mean, 80% of what we've used since the beginning of time has been marijuana.
We've used it for everything.
We've also been smoking it since the beginning of time.
Thomas Jefferson smoked it all the time.
That's probably why, even though he was racist,
He was fooling around with all the black ladies,
Because he probably made him horny and made him want to scoot around.
And he even says, you know, some of my best times were atop Monticello,
Looking into the, smoking the hemp plants, looking off into the sunset.
And all those guys are all stoners.
All the smartest, you know, most creative people in the world are stoners.
Sure, weed, if you smoke it too much, it will fuck you up.
I mean, I could tell.
I could go on and on and on and on about how weed has fucked me up.
But at the end of the day, how are you going to wait to war on something
That there's no way you can win?
There's no way you're going to win.
There's no way you can make people stop smoking marijuana if they smoked it since the beginning of time.
So what?
Are you going to, I mean, are you planning on having the war on drugs until the end of time?
Like, throw people in jail until the end of time?
Like, you're not going to win that war.
It's not going to be won.
You're going to lose.
So we'll just give up now.
$40 billion a year, I think, is what we spend on it.
Um, to throw people in jail.
So it's like, you know, people have all these cool ideas about how to spend our money, you know?
Oh, let's give tax incentives to people who put solar panels on their houses.
Oh, but where's the money going to come from?
We don't have the money.
Well, get rid of the war on drugs.
Use that money.
That's a lot of money.
And that's just the war on drugs.
What about all the money we put into our jails?
Um, and, uh, and, uh, and then all the fear that it creates.
It's like, you can't admit that you've smoked marijuana, that you even like to smoke marijuana.
It's kind of like somebody who's gay can't admit that they're gay.

Or somebody who believes in reincarnation can't believe that they can't say that they believe in reincarnation because, oh, no, then they could lose their job or, you know, something bad could happen, you know?
So that, so then they're going to be afraid of everything else.
You're going to, if you're afraid of one thing, you know, it's a gateway fear.
It'll make you fear of everything else.
So just fucking let people smoke the goddamn marijuana.
Let them grow it.
It's just a plant.
God made it.
Uh, you don't live in a society where everybody's afraid and everybody has to go to jail.
You don't, you don't make somebody do something by punishing them for doing something that you think is bad.
You make somebody do something by rewarding them for doing something that you know is right.
You know what I mean?
Like there's a crackhead.
Like I lived with a crackhead.
My brother-in-law was a crackhead.
I lived with him for three years.
Now, I wouldn't go, I wouldn't punch him in the face and tie his hands to the tree if he smelled crack.
I would just go, hey, let's work out.
Let's go mountain biking.
And then he'd be like, oh, no, I'm too hyped up on a crack.
I can't do that.
And I'd be like, well, then stop the crack for one day.
And we'll go mountain biking.
So then he goes mountain biking.
He feels great.
And then he comes home.
He doesn't feel like smoking crack.
Crack problem's over.
You know, you don't throw me in jail for selling crack or for selling marijuana just because of some other person smokes too much of it.
You know, you don't punish me to try to get them to not smoke it.
You know, it's not going to be one.
A war is going to be lost.
Hey, what about that movie cocaine cowboys?
All those people, Grisela lady killed 200 people.
All these other people killing each other.
They wouldn't have had the money to kill each other.
They wouldn't have gotten that rich if it wasn't for the drug trade.
They got their money out for the drug trade.
Why don't we tax the people for the drug trade for selling drugs?
Make drug dealers doing it legally.
And then we can use those revenues, tax those people, to put solar panels on people's houses.
You know what I mean?
Number one enemy to civilization is the fucking war on drugs.
So, what do you do about it?
I think everybody should talk about weed.
Everybody should go, yeah, 420 dude, yay.
Feeling themselves getting stoned.
I mean, pull out all stops.
Nobody has a sense of humor like a stoner.
Got to have a sense of humor about it.
Got to make fun of your stoniness and just slap these, you know, these control freaks around.
Just with your humor, because that will bring the demons out of them.
They'll be, ah, you should die.
I should kill you.
You're a piece of shit.
You suck.
You're stupid.
And then you go, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah, ah.
And then they go, uh, you're laughing at me and I'm so mad.
And all the demons leave.
And then when they're done, they go, oh man, that felt good.
You're letting it all out.
And then they relax.
And then you can realize that you're just a person too.
And everybody's just a person.
We're just humans here, trying to get along, you know?
And then, so that's what we have to do.
Stoners have to grow a fuckin' spine, man.
Gotta grow a spine.
Stoners gotta grow a spine.
You gotta get stoned.
You gotta get stoned on YouTube, man.
Hell yeah.
All right.
